question: In peptide-based electrochemical biosensors, what role do peptide recognition elements play?
options: They serve as conductive wires to transmit electrons to the electrode
They selectively bind target analytes, generating a measurable change in electrochemical signal
They catalyze the oxidation of the electrode surface
They prevent non-specific protein adsorption on the electrode
explanation: Peptide recognition elements (aptamers, molecular imprinted peptides, or receptor-binding peptides) selectively capture target analytes. Binding events alter electrochemical properties (impedance, current, or potential) at the electrode surface, enabling sensitive and specific detection.
